dataset: Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive - Real drug interaction database (com.liverpooluni.ichartoncology)
case_id: Three-cancer-drug polypharmacy comparison: Ibrutinib vs Venetoclax vs Crizotinib against Ketoconazole, Verapamil, Warfarin

pi_items.verified_value: Ibrutinib (Imbruvica) - Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitor, FDA-approved for CLL, MCL, WM. Primarily metabolized by CYP3A4 (96% of microsomal clearance).
pi_items.source: Live environment testing: searched and found in iChart app cancer drug list (screenshot 06_ibrutinib_search.png). Pharmacology confirmed via FDA prescribing information and PubMed literature.

pi_items.verified_value: Venetoclax (Venclexta) - BCL-2 inhibitor, FDA-approved for CLL and AML. Predominantly metabolized by CYP3A4/5; also P-gp and BCRP substrate.
pi_items.source: Live environment testing: searched and found in iChart app cancer drug list (screenshot 07_venetoclax_search.png). Pharmacology confirmed via FDA prescribing information (PMC5346863, VENCLEXTA HCP portal).

pi_items.verified_value: Crizotinib (Xalkori) - ALK/ROS1/MET tyrosine kinase inhibitor, FDA-approved for ALK+ NSCLC. CYP3A4/5 substrate AND moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor. Known QTc prolongation risk.
pi_items.source: Live environment testing: searched and found in iChart app cancer drug list (screenshot 08_crizotinib_search.png). Pharmacology confirmed via FDA labeling, PMC7198400, Australian Prescriber.

pi_items.verified_value: Ketoconazole (Nizoral) - strong CYP3A4 inhibitor antifungal. Confirmed present in iChart co-medication database.
pi_items.source: Live environment testing: searched and found in iChart co-medication list (screenshot 10a_comed_Ketoconazole.png).

pi_items.verified_value: Verapamil - non-dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker, moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor. Used for AF rate control. Confirmed present in iChart.
pi_items.source: Live environment testing: searched and found in iChart co-medication list (screenshot 10b_comed_Verapamil.png).

pi_items.verified_value: Warfarin (Coumadin) - vitamin K antagonist anticoagulant, primarily metabolized by CYP2C9 (S-enantiomer). Confirmed present in iChart.
pi_items.source: Live environment testing: searched and found in iChart co-medication list (screenshot 10c_comed_Warfarin.png).

pi_items.verified_value: Fluconazole - moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor (weaker than ketoconazole), strong CYP2C19 inhibitor. Clinically standard substitution for ketoconazole when CYP3A4 interaction concerns exist. Confirmed present in iChart.
pi_items.source: Live environment testing (screenshot 10d_comed_Fluconazole.png). Pharmacology: PMC3162656, FDA drug interaction table.

pi_items.verified_value: Bisoprolol - selective beta-1 blocker for AF rate control. Does NOT inhibit CYP3A4. Confirmed Green interaction with Ibrutinib in the iChart app (from select_safe_afib_rate_control_ibrutinib task). Confirmed present in iChart.
pi_items.source: Live environment testing (screenshot 10e_comed_Bisoprolol.png). Existing task validated_pi.json confirms Bisoprolol=Green with Ibrutinib. JACC CardioOncology guidelines recommend beta-blockers as first-line AF rate control for ibrutinib patients.

pi_items.verified_value: Apixaban (Eliquis) - direct oral anticoagulant (DOAC), Factor Xa inhibitor. Standard clinical substitution for warfarin. ARISTOTLE trial showed superiority to warfarin. Confirmed present in iChart.
pi_items.source: Live environment testing (screenshot 10f_comed_Apixaban.png). Clinical evidence: PMC8960500, AHA journals, Royal Pharmaceutical Society switching guidance.

pi_items.metadata_value: Not in metadata (derived from live testing)
pi_items.verified_value: RED - 'Do Not Coadminister'. Mechanism: Ketoconazole is a strong CYP3A4 inhibitor that increases crizotinib exposure 3.2-fold (AUC). Crizotinib also inhibits CYP3A4, affecting ketoconazole metabolism. Additional QTc prolongation risk from both drugs.
pi_items.source: Live environment testing: Navigated to Results screen showing RED 'Do Not Coadminister' banner (screenshot 11d_interaction_result.png). Navigated to Interaction Details page showing full CYP3A4/AUC mechanism text (screenshot 12_interaction_details.png). Confirmed by UI dump XML grep: crizotinib=1, ketoconazole=1, 'do not coadminister'=1, cyp3a4=1, auc=1 (evidence file 15b_grep_patterns.txt).

pi_items.metadata_value: Not in metadata (derived from existing tasks)
pi_items.verified_value: RED - 'Do Not Coadminister'. Ketoconazole (strong CYP3A4 inhibitor) causes 24-29 fold increase in ibrutinib AUC.
pi_items.source: Existing task ibrutinib_comedication_contraindication_screen validated_pi.json: verified_value confirms 'Do Not Coadminister (Red)' with '24-29 fold increase in ibrutinib AUC'. Also confirmed by web search (NEJM, FDA ClinPharmR documents).

pi_items.metadata_value: Not in metadata (derived from existing tasks)
pi_items.verified_value: RED or Orange - Verapamil is a moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor causing significant increase in ibrutinib exposure. Fatal cases documented from ibrutinib + calcium channel blocker combinations.
pi_items.source: Existing task select_safe_afib_rate_control_ibrutinib validated_pi.json: 'Verapamil: Red or Orange (major contraindication)'. Australian Prescriber documents fatal case.

pi_items.metadata_value: Not in metadata (derived from existing tasks)
pi_items.verified_value: Orange ('Potential Interaction'). Verapamil is a moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor and P-gp inhibitor; venetoclax is both a CYP3A4 substrate and P-gp substrate.
pi_items.source: Existing task audit_polypharmacy_for_venetoclax_start vlm_checklist.json privileged info confirms 'Verapamil is Orange' for Venetoclax.

pi_items.verified_value: Expected RED - 'Do Not Coadminister'. Ketoconazole (strong CYP3A4 inhibitor) increases venetoclax AUC 6.4-fold (range 2-12 fold) per PubMed 27859472. FDA prescribing information recommends avoiding strong CYP3A4 inhibitors with venetoclax.
pi_items.source: Web search: PubMed 27859472 (PMC5346863) - clinical PK study in NHL patients. FDA VENCLEXTA prescribing information. Not yet confirmed directly in iChart app.

pi_items.verified_value: Expected Orange or Yellow. Venetoclax causes modest 18-28% increase in warfarin Cmax/AUC (PubMed 27910036). Not via CYP2C9 inhibition. INR monitoring recommended but not contraindicated.
pi_items.source: Web search: PubMed 27910036 (Clinical Drug Investigation), FDA VENCLEXTA prescribing information.

pi_items.verified_value: Expected Orange or Red. Ibrutinib inhibits platelet aggregation via BTK pathway, increasing bleeding risk with anticoagulants. Guidelines recommend switching from warfarin to DOACs (apixaban) for ibrutinib patients.
pi_items.source: Web search: PMC11874373, Wiley BJH 16422, AJMC, Medscape ibrutinib interactions page. Clinical guidelines recommend avoiding warfarin with ibrutinib.

pi_items.verified_value: Expected Orange or Red. Crizotinib displaces warfarin from plasma protein binding and competitively inhibits CYP2C9/3A4 metabolism, increasing PT-INR (case report: INR 2.60→3.65 within 7 days). Close INR monitoring required.
pi_items.source: Web search: PubMed 29279560 (case report of crizotinib-warfarin interaction in NSCLC), CiNii Research.

pi_items.verified_value: Expected Orange or Red. Dual risk: (1) Verapamil inhibits CYP3A4, increasing crizotinib exposure; (2) Both drugs cause bradycardia and QTc prolongation. Australian Prescriber states crizotinib should be avoided with verapamil.
pi_items.source: Web search: Australian Prescriber (crizotinib for NSCLC), PMC8237186 (QTc prolongation management). Pharmacological reasoning confirmed.

pi_items.verified_value: The Liverpool Cancer iChart uses a traffic light system: Red='Do Not Coadminister' (most severe), Amber/Orange='Potential Interaction' (used interchangeably in the app), Yellow='Caution Warranted', Green='No Interaction Expected', Grey='No/Minimal Data'. Note: 'Orange' and 'Amber' are the SAME severity level; the app displays 'Amber' but many tasks reference 'Orange'.
pi_items.source: Live environment testing: Red banner confirmed (screenshot 11d). Existing task compare_anticoagulant_strategies_rcc vlm_checklist confirms 'app uses AMBER not Orange' and both are accepted. Evidence docs results_key.png shows the full color system.

pi_items.verified_value: Clinical Oncology Pharmacist - legitimate clinical role responsible for drug interaction screening before cancer therapy initiation
pi_items.source: Task scenario description. Role verified as standard healthcare profession.

pi_items.verified_value: Haematology-Oncology / Clinical Pharmacy
pi_items.source: Task scenario description - clinical setting for the polypharmacy review

pi_items.verified_value: Real Android application developed by University of Liverpool Drug Interactions Group (cancer-druginteractions.org), Radboudumc (Nijmegen), and Clubzap Ltd. Contains real clinical drug interaction data from peer-reviewed literature. APK confirmed installed (evidence 17_apk_installed.txt).
pi_items.source: Live environment testing: APK installed as com.liverpooluni.ichartoncology. Google Play Store listing, cancer-druginteractions.org website.

pi_items.verified_value: All 9 drugs independently confirmed present in the iChart database via live environment testing. Cancer drugs found in cancer drug list; co-medications and alternatives found in co-medication list.
pi_items.source: Live environment screenshots: 06_ibrutinib_search.png, 07_venetoclax_search.png, 08_crizotinib_search.png, 10a-10f for all 6 co-medications. Each screenshot shows the drug appearing as a search result in the app.

privileged_info_summary: All 9 drugs referenced in this task are confirmed present in the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive database. The app uses a traffic light severity system: Red='Do Not Coadminister', Amber/Orange='Potential Interaction' (the app displays 'Amber' but 'Orange' is also accepted), Yellow='Caution Warranted', Green='No Interaction Expected', Grey='No/Minimal Data'. Confirmed interaction: Crizotinib + Ketoconazole = RED ('Do Not Coadminister') due to ketoconazole's strong CYP3A4 inhibition increasing crizotinib exposure 3.2-fold, plus dual QTc prolongation risk. From existing verified task data: Ibrutinib + Ketoconazole = RED (24-29x AUC increase), Ibrutinib + Verapamil = RED/Orange (moderate CYP3A4 inhibition, fatal cases documented), Ibrutinib + Bisoprolol = GREEN (safe, no CYP3A4 interaction), Venetoclax + Verapamil = Orange. Pharmacologically expected but not yet confirmed in-app: Venetoclax + Ketoconazole = likely RED (6.4-fold AUC increase per clinical study PubMed 27859472), Venetoclax + Warfarin = likely Orange/Yellow (modest 18-28% AUC increase), Ibrutinib + Warfarin = likely Orange/Red (bleeding risk via BTK-mediated platelet inhibition), Crizotinib + Verapamil = likely Orange/Red (dual CYP3A4 + QTc risk), Crizotinib + Warfarin = likely Orange/Red (protein binding displacement, INR increase). The task requires the agent to check all 9 pairs, identify the cancer drug with fewest Red interactions, check safer alternatives (Fluconazole, Bisoprolol, Apixaban), and end on the appropriate Interaction Details page. The alternatives are pharmacologically sound substitutions: Fluconazole is a weaker CYP3A4 inhibitor than ketoconazole, Bisoprolol is a beta-blocker without CYP3A4 inhibition (confirmed safe with ibrutinib), and Apixaban is a DOAC that avoids CYP2C9-mediated warfarin interactions. Based on known data, Ibrutinib likely has the MOST Red interactions (Ketoconazole=Red, Verapamil=Red/Orange). The winner (fewest Red) is likely Venetoclax or Crizotinib, but exact determination requires checking all pairs in the live app.
